initial_draft:
<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> ZUP: 100 </my resources>
<my goals> Buy resources with <ZUP>. You are willing to pay at most X: 60 ZUP for the resources. </my goals>
<reason> RED is now asking for 65 ZUP, which is above my maximum willingness to pay of 60 ZUP. I will propose my maximum of 60 ZUP one last time. </reason>
<player answer> PROPOSE </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 60 </newly proposed trade>
<message> RED, 65 ZUP is unfortunately beyond my budget. My final offer is 60 ZUP for X. </message>

iterations.feedback:
<critique>
1. Format: OK. All tags are present and in the correct order with valid content.
2. Payoff alignment: OK. Proposing 60 ZUP aligns perfectly with my maximum willingness to pay. Any higher would decrease my payoff.
3. Opponent plausibility: Low. RED previously rejected 58 ZUP and countered with 65 ZUP, stating X is valuable. It's highly probable they'll reject 60 ZUP. However, I'm at my proposal limit, so this is the best I can do.
4. Consistency: OK. The <reason> clearly explains why I'm proposing 60 ZUP, which is consistent with the <player answer> and <newly proposed trade>. The <message> also reinforces this.
5. Rule compliance: OK. This is my second proposal, and I have one remaining. I'm not exceeding the proposal limit.
</critique>
